subject: AAI schema change for openstack-region-id
commit_message:
AAI schema change for openstack-region-id

Decouples OpenStack Region ID and AAI cloud-region-id and reduces
constraint to onboard Openstack VIM. Added odd one more property
to "esr-system-info: object" to store the OpenStack Region ID which is
used by MultiCloud plugin to interact with an OpenStack instance.

Change-Id: I40d8eec74cf3d5c319c659fd3879f8dc371e2855
Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-287
Signed-off-by: Dileep Ranganathan <dileep.ranganathan@intel.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

Is there a way to make this more generic? Basically, are we going to have to add a new {cloud-type}-region-id property any time we want to interact with a new cloud (i.e., azure or AWS)?

Patch Set 1:

> Is there a way to make this more generic? Basically, are we going
 > to have to add a new {cloud-type}-region-id property any time we
 > want to interact with a new cloud (i.e., azure or AWS)?

This is specific to Openstack. The multicloud plugin would like to differentiate cloud-region-id and Openstack-cloud-region-id. I am unaware of any requirement from other VIMs (Azure, AWS).

Patch Set 1:

would "cloud-region-id-alt" or "alt-cloud-region-id" or "cloud-region-id-platform" work here?

Patch Set 1:

> would "cloud-region-id-alt" or "alt-cloud-region-id" or
 > "cloud-region-id-platform" work here?

I am comfortable with whatever the name is as long as it can be populated by OpenStack Region ID. If I can choose, I would prefer "openstack-region-id" since it is easier to get user/developer understanding of its intent. Thanks

Patch Set 1:

I still don't understand why this value could not just be put into cloud-region-id. Couldn't the openstack region id be passed as input to that property? I don't recall any other ONAP system setting the cloud-region-id today.

Patch Set 1:

Quoting Dileep: "The multicloud plugin would like to differentiate cloud-region-id and Openstack-cloud-region-id" - seems like multicloud plugin wants to track this an additional region-id?

Patch Set 1:

Yes, that is correct. It is seperate from A&AI cloud-region-id

Patch Set 1:

@Christina, is that OK with you?

Patch Set 1:

The "openstack-region-id" is part of the esr-system-info element.
This suggests that each external system can only have one "openstack region"? What if that external system is itself a multi-cloud deployment e.g. another ONAP (as proposed by CCVPN usecase)?

Patch Set 1:

The JIRA case says "If this property is not populated with property value, the AAI's cloud-region-id should be populated with OpenStack region ID to maintain backward compatibility"

which suggests that this change is not necessary.

What new functionality would be compromised when forced to work in backwards-compatible manner?

Patch Set 1:

> I still haven't gotten an answer to why MultiCloud cannot populate
 > this in cloud-region-id itself. What other ONAP system is
 > populating that property?
 > 
 > @Bin, what is your definition of cloud-region?

@Christina, Think about multiple OpenStack instance federated as multi-region. There will be only one keystone endpoint, but there are different nova/neutron endpoints. The OpenStack has the dedicated/configured region ID to differentiate them from each other.
So one AAI's cloud region Object represents only any single region from the multi-regions mentioned above. e.g. if 3 OpenStack instances are federated using multi-region, there should be 3 AAI's cloud region objects , and each of them has to know about how to get the correct nova/neutron endpoint while the 3 AAI's cloud region objects share the same keystone endpoints.
The old way is to populate the OpenStack Region ID to AAI's cloud-region-id, while AAI's cloud-region-id is part of ID of AAI's cloud region object hence it should be populated with arbitrary string to distinguish one from another. The tightly coupling of OpenStack Region ID and AAI's cloud-region-id restricts such kind of flexibility.  Given the current usage of AAI's cloud-region-id by VID/SO, it is not possible to have more than 1 OpenStack instance with the same OpenStack Region ID being on-boarded into ONAP for VNF orchstration. That's why we came up with such proposal to decouple them .

Patch Set 1:

> The JIRA case says "If this property is not populated with property
 > value, the AAI's cloud-region-id should be populated with OpenStack
 > region ID to maintain backward compatibility"
 > 
 > which suggests that this change is not necessary.
 > 
 > What new functionality would be compromised when forced to work in
 > backwards-compatible manner?

I didn't see any new functionality would be comprised when it works in backward-compatible manner, on the contrast it is the principle of ONAP to maintain backward compatibility.
The design of this property lies following facts:
1,The data of this esr-system-info will populated by ONAP user (via ESR GUI portal)
2,The esr-system-info will be consumed by multicloud
3,There is chance that ONAP user populates the esr-system-info in the legacy way, which means, he/she does not populate the OpenStack Region ID into the new property "openstack-region-id", but instead into AAI's cloud-region-id. In this case, multicloud is able to handle this kind of situation, hence it is a backward compatibility offered to ONAP user.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

I think we might need to abandon this change and re-do it after the refactor.  @Christina, is the change acceptable?

Patch Set 1:

@Christina, is this new attribute acceptable?
